text: I feel oddly disoriented standing here—both by the askew angles of these rooms and by the drifting, almost out-of-sync conversation. There’s an emptiness heightened by the sterile corporate design and the distant glow of blue exit signs. The second space, saturated in abstract colors and technical jargon about humans and machines, adds to my abstraction. I am left reflecting on the uncanny nature of human perception: how easily a comment or a display can unsettle or provoke thought. With popcorn in hand, I sense my own detachment increasing, yet curiosity remains—am I merely observing, or am I part of this subtle unease?
